metadata_information.title: Urban Form and Productivity : What Is the Shape of Latin American Cities
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Urban Form and Productivity : What Is the Shape of Latin American Cities

document_description.abstract: This paper examines the linkages between urban form and city productivity, using alternative metrics for urban form and applying them to a comprehensive sample of Latin American cities. Although most of the literature has concentrated on the effects of population density (compact versus sprawling urban development), this paper seeks to assess whether different dimensions of a city's urban form, such as shape, structure, and land-use, affect its economic performance. The paper finds that the shape of the urban extent, the inner-city connectedness, and fullness have a statistically significant influence on the productivity level of the city.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
